title: WHY DONATE? AND TO WHOM? - CASE STUDY | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So we have concept of donations. In every religion there is a concept where the people who are
practicing that particular religion are encouraged to donate, to contribute, to do some seva.
And many religions have that in an organized form. For example, in Christianity, anyone can help me
with an answer. What is it called? Taith, yes. Where every devout Christian is encouraged if he
is part of the church attending the Sunday Mass, 10% of what he earns he gives to the church.
In fact,
I was speaking to one gentleman. I was traveling with him and I happened to strike a conversation
with him. And then I asked him, have you visited our temple in Bangalore? So then he said, no,
I'm a Christian. I haven't visited your temple. So then we continued our talk. And then he says,
I go to the church. And then he reminded me of the Saint Bethel's Church.
Which is very close to our place. And then I said, you know, I started asking a few things
about him, his religion and what the Bible says. So then I was curious to know about this concept
of Taith. So I asked him, is this a fact? All those who are very strongly associated with the
church, do they really follow this? And is this true? So he said, yes, I'm a Christian. And then I
had not visited the church. You are supposed to do that, 10% of what you earn,
you are supposed to contribute to the church. He corrected me. In fact, he says it's not just 10%
of what we earn. The right understanding is 10% of everything, including my time.
So he says, every Sunday, I'm there in the church and I spend about 7

Four ways we can serve the Lord.
Pranayama, Arthayar. Arthayar means with the money.
Why money? Not that God wants our money.
God is not poor.
In fact, the money what we have belongs to God.
It is coming from God. It is God.

because we are attached to money,
all the work we do gets translated as a result

we work morning to evening,
five days, six days a week.

There are three kinds of activities.
That's why I said I'll add my two bit of insights into what you're contributing.
There are Papa. Papa means sinful activities.
The result of sinful activities is suffering in this world.
Then there is Paapam.
Punya means pious activity.
Opening an orphanage, feeding a poor underprivileged.
The result of Punya or pious activity is good reaction because of which we enjoy in this material world.
Janma, Aishwarya, Shrutashri.
We get good birth, we get opulence, prosperity, Shrutah, good education.
That's the result of Punya activities.
But Papa or Punya has a reaction.
And reaction, good or bad, in this material world, you have to take birth to enjoy or suffer the reactions.
When we serve the Lord, that action has neither good reaction or not bad reaction in the material domain.
It has the highest reaction in a spiritual domain which elevates the soul.
So it is not Punya.
It is much, much, much, much higher than Punya.

living in you know, very palatial house with all comforts, all luxuries, still he grows old, still he dies.
So, when we engage in spiritual activities, service of the Lord, that activity is called Akarma.
Punya Karma, Papa Karma and Akarma.
That does not give us a material reaction, it gives us a spiritual reaction.
And that spiritual reaction is for the soul.
Material reaction gives us good body, good intelligence in a material domain, good birth.
Is that clear? So, it is not highest Punya, it is greatest, which gives us transcendental results for spiritual emancipation.
